title: Sandwiches
content:
SANDWICHES

TYPES

Closed: Filling between two slices
Open-Faced: Filling on single slice; served without top

INGREDIENTS

Bread: White, brown, rye, whole wheat, crackers, rolls, buns. Day-old bread cuts best. Sandwich loaves cut to advantage.

Fillings: Chopped/shredded meats, vegetables, fish, nuts added to cream cheese, salad dressings, butter. Slices of cheese, well-seasoned meat, fish, vegetables. Sweet or salted butter with nuts or dried fruits.

Carriers: Lettuce, endive, watercress prevent soggy bread and add color.

PREPARATION

Trim crusts if desired (trim loaf before slicing). Butter should be softened by creaming; spread on loaf before each slice cut.

Thickness: Home service—⅓ inch thick; crusts not removed; cut in half (straight or diagonal). Party sandwiches—less than ¼ inch thick; crusts removed; may cut fancy shapes.

Toasting: Some sandwiches better if bread toasted before adding filling. Others toasted after filling added. Serve toasted sandwiches immediately.

Commercially Prepared: Sandwich spreads, salad dressings, fillings available for convenience and piquant flavor.
